title: BBG Originals previews Sudan documentary showing humanitarian crisis in Darfur refugee camps
summary: BBG Originals announced a forthcoming investigative documentary covering deteriorating conditions in Darfur displacement camps, featuring interviews with aid workers and displaced families amid the ongoing RSF-SAF conflict.
contents:
# BBG Originals Previews Darfur Humanitarian Crisis Documentary

## Documentary Announcement
BBG Originals shared a preview of its upcoming investigative documentary examining the humanitarian catastrophe unfolding in western Sudan's Darfur region.

## Coverage Focus
- Three months of field reporting from displacement camps near El Fasher
- Interviews with 40+ displaced families documenting systematic violence
- Aid worker testimonies describing collapsing medical infrastructure
- Satellite imagery analysis showing destruction of civilian areas

## Humanitarian Context
- Over 2.5 million displaced in Darfur since the RSF-SAF conflict escalated in April 2023
- Food insecurity affects 85% of camp populations
- Medical facilities operating at 15% capacity due to supply shortages
- Children under five face acute malnutrition rates exceeding emergency thresholds

## Strategic Significance
The documentary provides ground-level evidence of systematic targeting of civilian populations and infrastructure destruction, supporting calls for international accountability mechanisms.

## Release Timeline
Full documentary scheduled for release next week across multiple platforms.
